title: lepidaploa obtusifolia
intro: Lepidaploa obtusifolia, also known as the rough-leaved lepidaploa, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Lepidaploa obtusifolia have been associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or decoction, often combined with other herbs, to create teas and tinctures that are used in folk medicine.
